exercises.title: Deduplicate and Test Membership
exercises.description: Remove duplicates from a list of emails while preserving order, then test membership. Also count how many unique emails there are.
exercises.starterCode:
emails = [
    "alice@example.com",
    "bob@example.com",
    "alice@example.com",
    "carol@example.com",
    "bob@example.com",
    "david@example.com",
]

# TODO: Create 'unique_emails' list with duplicates removed, preserving order
# Hint: use dict.fromkeys() or a loop with a seen set

# TODO: Print the number of unique emails

# TODO: Print whether 'alice@example.com' is in unique_emails (True/False)

# TODO: Print whether 'eve@example.com' is in unique_emails (True/False)

print(unique_emails)
hints: list(dict.fromkeys(emails)) removes duplicates while preserving order

exercises.title: Set Operations: Course Enrollment
exercises.description: Given two sets of students in different courses, use set operations to find: students in both courses, students only in Python, students only in Web Dev, and all students combined.
exercises.starterCode:
python_students = {"Alice", "Bob", "Carol", "David", "Eve"}
web_students = {"Carol", "David", "Frank", "Grace"}

# TODO: Find students enrolled in BOTH courses (intersection)

# TODO: Find students ONLY in Python (difference)

# TODO: Find students ONLY in Web Dev (difference)

# TODO: Find ALL unique students across both courses (union)

print("Both:", sorted(both))
print("Python only:", sorted(python_only))
print("Web only:", sorted(web_only))
print("All students:", len(all_students))
exercises.expectedOutput:
Both: ['Carol', 'David']
Python only: ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Eve']
Web only: ['Frank', 'Grace']
All students: 7
hints: difference: python_students - web_students gives Python-only students
difference: web_students - python_students gives Web-only students
union: python_students | web_students gives all students

exercises.title: Set Comprehension: Unique First Letters
exercises.description: Use a set comprehension to find all unique first letters from a list of names. Then check if all names starting with 'A' are present by verifying 'A' is in the set.
exercises.starterCode:
names = ["Alice", "Bob", "Carol", "Alan", "Betty", "Charlie", "Anna"]

# TODO: Create 'first_letters' using a set comprehension
# Each element should be the first character of each name

# TODO: Print the sorted list of first letters

# TODO: Print how many unique first letters there are

# TODO: Print whether 'D' is in first_letters (True/False)
hints: {name[0] for name in names} creates a set of first characters
